what_it_does: Reframes the merge of three memory-related systems (episodic-memory, Company corpus, substrate-mcp) as a climb from sub-floor current states to a unified 'one living memory' intention for the Company entity, using Tim's 2026-06-11 lens and a code-grounded synthesis.
observations: Tim's correction: all three current systems sit below a minimum floor, not strong things to combine.
The conversation store is the sole primary source; every other corpus is a generated projection of the transcripts.
A latent provenance spine exists: tool_calls.tool_input carries artifact addresses, joining generated artifacts back to exchange/session/timestamp.
The dormancy frame: all generated content is unused production material; this work is the inflection point where it becomes active memory.
Three faculties are defined: capture (episodic-memory), understanding (Company corpus), metabolism (substrate-mcp).
The shared relational primitive is the exchange://<sid>/<i> junction.
An upgrade ledger maps current → floor → intention across capture, understanding, metabolism, embedding, store, retrieval, and lifecycle.
Open decisions D1–D5 and new decisions N1–N3 are explicitly surfaced rather than assumed away.
The synthesis section concludes that almost everything is wiring/activation of existing pieces, with one big build (Postgres+pgvector resolver) and a short net-new list.
A superseded note shows D4 (embedder unification on bge-m3) was overridden by Tim's 2026-06-13 directive.
standouts: The 'all sub-floor' altitude-stack reframing and the CURRENT/FLOOR/INTENTION structure.
The provenance graph claim: 6.7M tool_calls with artifact addresses make the conversation store the spine of all generated content.
The verdict that the one genuinely large net-new build is a Postgres+pgvector resolver extension along the existing Resolver Protocol.
The short net-new list versus the large volume of already-built but dormant machinery.
The turn-context embedding unit from conversation-intelligence as a richer vector primitive than raw exchange.
conventions: Uses CURRENT → FLOOR → INTENTION structure for each body.
Labels claims as Observed/Inferred and marks intention sections as tentative for Tim correction.

Relies heavily on internal project codenames and laws (feedback-no-episodic-memory, project-company-one-entity, common-memory-temporal, introspective-data-building, altitude-transformation-layer, G23, GC14).
Includes provenance/status annotations, including a superseded block.
Uses ASCII diagrams and markdown tables.
References companion docs in the same folder rather than duplicating them.
concerns: Large portions are explicitly tentative and await Tim correction, so the doc is provisional.
Dense internal terminology may be opaque without the companion docs.
The superseded D4 note creates a temporal inconsistency within the same file.
The scope is enormous (75k exchanges, 114k chunks, growing model layer) and sequencing could be clearer.
notes: A strategic framing doc that says the memory merge is not about combining three working systems but about lifting all three from below a minimum floor toward one living Company memory, with a code-grounded synthesis identifying mostly wiring plus a Postgres+pgvector store backend.
questions: What is the exact filesystem layout of the Company store and how does the Resolver Protocol map to a Postgres schema?
Which embedder will be chosen now that Tim's directive says bge-m3 is only one unranked loadout?
What is the precise operational definition of 'fabric-grade' embedding and the fabric model layer?
How will 'proposes_only' governance be enforced across both raw and distilled memory layers?
What is the intended user or API surface for the unified memory once built?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to provide a corrected merge framing for Tim's steer; actual content does that and also synthesizes code reads into a concrete build plan, so it matches and exceeds the stated purpose.
apparent_themes: Sub-floor honesty: no existing system is good enough as-is.
Inflection from dormant generated material to active memory.
One entity, one memory (continuity across sessions).
intention_signals: It is trying to align a scattered set of memory prototypes under a single, higher ambition: give the Company entity a unified, self-improving episodic and knowledge memory rooted in conversation provenance, while being honest about current gaps and the minimal real new construction required.
novelty: The 'all sub-floor' altitude stack and the explicit CURRENT/FLOOR/INTENTION separation, plus treating the conversation store as the sole primary source and everything else as regenerable projection, is a distinctive reframing of a multi-system merge.
contribution: Provides the strategic framing, the provenance-spine concept, the exchange:// junction primitive, the faculty decomposition (capture/understanding/metabolism), the upgrade ledger, and a code-grounded synthesis that narrows the merge to a short net-new build list.
summary_for_embedding: MERGE-INTENTION.md reframes the episodic-memory/Company-corpus/substrate-mcp merge as lifting three sub-floor systems toward one living Company memory rooted in conversation provenance, with a code-grounded synthesis identifying mostly wiring and a Postgres+pgvector resolver as the one big build.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to correct the merge framing per Tim's 2026-06-11 steer and produce a tentative, code-grounded intention for a unified, self-improving Company memory.
